lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਥਿਆ ਚਰਨ ਪਰ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਕਉ ਧਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: mithiaa charan par bikaar kau dhaaveh |
lines.english: False are the feet which run to do evil to others.
False is the tongue which enjoys delicacies and external tastes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਥਿਆ ਨੇਤ੍ਰ ਪੇਖਤ ਪਰ ਤ੍ਰਿਅ ਰੂਪਾਦ ॥
lines.translit: mithiaa netr pekhat par tria roopaad |
lines.english: False are the eyes which gaze upon the beauty of another's wife.
False is the body which does not do good to others.
False is the nose which inhales corruption.
False is the mind which covets the wealth of others.
The life of the faithless cynic is totally useless.
Without understanding, everything is false.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਫਲ ਦੇਹ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਲਏ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: safal deh naanak har har naam le |5|
lines.english: Fruitful is the body, O Nanak, which takes to the Lord's Name. ||5||
Useless is the body of the spiritually blind, without the Name of the Lord.
Without the Truth, how can anyone be pure?
From his mouth, a foul smell issues forth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਦਿਨੁ ਰੈਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਬਿਹਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin simaran din rain brithaa bihaae |
lines.english: Without the remembrance of the Lord, day and night pass in vain,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੋਬਿਦ ਭਜਨ ਬਿਨੁ ਬ੍ਰਿਥੇ ਸਭ ਕਾਮ ॥
lines.translit: gobid bhajan bin brithe sabh kaam |
lines.english: Without meditation on the Lord of the Universe, all works are in vain,
like the crop which withers without rain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੰਨਿ ਧੰਨਿ ਤੇ ਜਨ ਜਿਹ ਘਟਿ ਬਸਿਓ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: dhan dhan te jan jih ghatt basio har naau |
lines.english: Blessed, blessed are those, whose hearts are filled with the Name of the Lord.
like the wealth of a miser, which lies useless.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਾ ਕੈ ਬਲਿ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਉ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: naanak taa kai bal bal jaau |6|
lines.english: Nanak is a sacrifice, a sacrifice to them. ||6||
He says one thing, and does something else.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਨਹੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਮੁਖਹੁ ਗੰਢ ਲਾਵਤ ॥
lines.translit: man nahee preet mukhahu gandt laavat |
lines.english: There is no love in his heart, and yet with his mouth he talks tall.
The Omniscient Lord God is the Knower of all.
One who does not practice what he preaches to others,
He is not impressed by outward display.
shall come and go in reincarnation, through birth and death.
One whose inner being is filled with the Formless Lord
Nanak falls at their feet. ||7||
by his teachings, the world is saved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੁਮ ਭਾਨੇ ਤਿਨ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਜਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: jo tum bhaane tin prabh jaataa |
lines.english: Those who are pleasing to You, God, know You.
He Himself, by Himself, makes the decisions.
Offer your prayers to the Supreme Lord God, who knows everything.
He Himself values His own creatures.
He is beyond all efforts and clever tricks.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਸੈ ਦੂਰਿ ਜਨਾਵਤ ਕਿਸੈ ਬੁਝਾਵਤ ਨੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kisai door janaavat kisai bujhaavat neraa |
lines.english: To some, He appears far away, while others perceive Him near at hand.
Those upon whom He bestows His favor, become His servants.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕਛੁ ਜਾਨੈ ਆਤਮ ਕੀ ਰਹਤ ॥
lines.translit: sabh kachh jaanai aatam kee rehat |
lines.english: He knows all the ways and means of the soul.
He is pervading all places and interspaces.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਲਏ ਲੜਿ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jis bhaavai tis le larr laae |
lines.english: Those with whom He is pleased are attached to the hem of His robe.
Salok:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮ ਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਅਰੁ ਲੋਭ ਮੋਹ ਬਿਨਸਿ ਜਾਇ ਅਹੰਮੇਵ ॥
lines.translit: kaam krodh ar lobh moh binas jaae ahamev |
lines.english: Sexual desire, anger, greed and emotional attachment - may these be gone, and egotism as well.
Each and every moment, O Nanak, meditate on the Lord. ||8||5||
By His Grace, you partake of the thirty-six delicacies;
Ashtapadee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਸਰਣਾਗਤੀ ਕਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦੁ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak prabh saranaagatee kar prasaad guradev |1|
lines.english: Nanak seeks the Sanctuary of God; please bless me with Your Grace, O Divine Guru. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਪਰਮ ਗਤਿ ਪਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tis kau simarat param gat paaveh |
lines.english: remembering Him, the supreme status is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕਉ ਰਖੁ ਮਨ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tis tthaakur kau rakh man maeh |
lines.english: enshrine that Lord and Master within your mind.
By His Grace, you apply scented oils to your body;
meditate forever on Him within your mind.
By His Grace, you dwell in the palace of peace;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਸੰਗਿ ਸੁਖ ਬਸਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: jih prasaad grih sang sukh basanaa |
lines.english: By His Grace, you abide with your family in peace;
By His Grace, you enjoy tastes and pleasures;
keep His remembrance upon your tongue, twenty-four hours a day.
why abandon Him, to attach yourself to another?
O Nanak, meditate forever on the One, who is worthy of meditation. ||1||
By His Grace, you wear silks and satins;
By His Grace, you sleep in a cozy bed;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਤੁਝੁ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਊ ਮਾਨੈ ॥
lines.translit: jih prasaad tujh sabh koaoo maanai |
lines.english: By His Grace, you are honored by everyone;

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਆਠ ਪਹਰ ਤਾ ਕਾ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਵੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: man aatth pehar taa kaa jas gaaveejai |
lines.english: O my mind, sing His Praises, twenty-four hours a day.
